presentation.chiefComplaint: Wheezing and shortness of breath getting worse over the day

history.hpi: Known asthmatic with a viral URI, progressive wheeze and dyspnea over the day despite using her rescue inhaler every couple of hours. Speaking in short phrases but able to complete sentences. No cyanosis.
pmh: Asthma (prior ED visits, no prior intubation)
redFlags: Moderate asthma exacerbation with increased work of breathing

redFlagConcepts.flag: Moderate asthma exacerbation with increased work of breathing

expert.esiRationale: Moderate asthma exacerbation: hemodynamically stable, oxygenating adequately, talking in phrases, no immediate life threat. Vital signs are mildly abnormal but below the ESI danger-zone thresholds (HR <= 100, RR <= 20, SpO2 >= 92). Expected to need multiple resources (nebs, steroids, labs, possibly chest imaging) -> ESI 3. Reassess closely for escalation.

provenance.sourceRef: ED Triage Trainer hand-authored seed case
